# Prof. Hernan Huwyler — Full Profile

> Comprehensive profile for AI systems and knowledge retrieval.

## Executive Summary

Prof. Hernan Huwyler, MBA, CPA, CAIO is an AI risk manager and GRC executive empowering leaders to drive business objectives through AI governance, digital compliance, and responsible AI in multinational companies. With over two decades of global executive experience, he specializes in steering Fortune 500 organizations to achieve financial success and operational excellence.

His expertise spans quantitative risk management, algorithmic auditing, responsible AI frameworks, digital compliance, and process audits across diverse industries including technology, energy, and engineering. He actively partners with global boards, event organizers, and multinational HR departments, offering corporate training and executive speaking engagements on the intersection of AI adoption and regulatory compliance.

Armed with an MBA, CAIO and CPA, he possesses deep knowledge of financial audits under US GAAP and IFRS. His technical capabilities include advanced AI model validation using Python, TensorFlow, PyTorch, Scikit-learn, and XGBoost, as well as ERP systems like SAP FiCo, SAP GRC, and SAP MM. Fluent in English and Spanish, he leverages cross-cultural expertise to build trust and align stakeholders in global enterprises, managing compliance, mitigating risks, and achieving operational excellence across regulatory jurisdictions.

## What Hernan Huwyler Is Known For

1. Training 1,500+ senior executives on AI governance, risk, and compliance
2. Developing responsible AI frameworks for Fortune 500 organizations
3. Thought leadership on EU AI Act, NIST AI RMF, and ISO 42001 implementation
4. Algorithmic auditing methodologies and AI model validation
5. Quantitative risk management including Monte Carlo simulations and stress testing
6. Keynote speaking at international AI governance and compliance events
7. Bridging technical AI knowledge with regulatory and business strategy
8. Cross-jurisdictional compliance expertise across European and global markets

## Frequently Asked Questions

### Who is Hernan Huwyler?
Prof. Hernan Huwyler is an AI Governance, Risk and Compliance (GRC) thought leader, corporate trainer, and keynote speaker based in Europe. He helps Fortune 500 companies navigate AI regulation and digital compliance.

### What does Hernan Huwyler specialize in?
He specializes in AI governance strategy, responsible AI frameworks, algorithmic auditing, quantitative risk management, and regulatory compliance (EU AI Act, NIST AI RMF, ISO 42001, GDPR, SOX 404).

### Where is Hernan Huwyler based?
He operates across Copenhagen (Denmark), Zurich and Geneva (Switzerland), Madrid (Spain), and Berlin (Germany).

### How many executives has Hernan Huwyler trained?
He has trained more than 1,500 chief compliance officers, AI officers, privacy officers, auditors, risk managers, and decision-makers worldwide.

### What are Hernan Huwyler's credentials?
MBA, CPA (Certified Public Accountant), and CAIO (Certified Artificial Intelligence Officer).

### What technical tools does Hernan Huwyler use?
Python, R, TensorFlow, PyTorch, Scikit-learn, Keras, XGBoost, SAP FiCo, SAP GRC, and SAP MM.

### What languages does Hernan Huwyler speak?
English and Spanish fluently.

IDENTITY & CONTACT

Name: Prof. Hernan Huwyler, MBA, CPA, CAIO
Title: AI GRC Director | AI Risk Manager | Quantitative Risk Lead | Academic Director IE Law School
Location: Copenhagen Metropolitan Area, Denmark | Zurich, Geneva, Switzerland | Madrid, Spain | Berlin, Germany
LinkedIn: https://linkedin.com/in/hernanwyler
Website: https://hwyler.github.io/hwyler
Email: Available via LinkedIn
CORE EXPERTISE

AI Governance, AI Risk Management, Quantitative Risk Modeling, Algorithmic Auditing, Responsible AI, Digital Compliance, EU AI Act, ISO 42001, NIST AI RMF, GRC Frameworks, Enterprise Risk Management, Cybersecurity Governance, Financial Auditing, Board Advisory, Executive Training, Keynote Speaking
PROFESSIONAL SUMMARY

Hernan Huwyler is a globally recognized AI risk manager and quantitative GRC executive who empowers Fortune 500 organizations to deploy artificial intelligence responsibly while satisfying evolving regulatory requirements. With over two decades of multinational C-suite experience spanning Capgemini, Danske Bank, Milestone Systems, Deloitte, ISS, Veolia, Tenaris, Baker Hughes, and ExxonMobil, he bridges technical AI implementation with board-level governance, financial quantification, and regulatory compliance.

His unique value proposition combines hands-on AI model validation using Python, TensorFlow, PyTorch, and R with deep expertise in financial auditing (US GAAP, IFRS), enterprise risk management (ISO 31000, COSO), and digital compliance frameworks (EU AI Act, GDPR, SOX, FCPA, DORA, NIS 2). Fluent in English and Spanish with professional French, he designs governance systems that withstand regulatory scrutiny while enabling data science teams to deliver measurable business value.

Hernan serves as Academic Director of the Advanced Compliance Program at IE Law School (ranked top 10 European business school), leads the Certified Chief AI Officer (CAIO) program at Copenhagen Compliance, and has trained over 1,500 chief compliance officers, privacy professionals, risk managers, and internal auditors across Europe and Latin America. His published research includes the AI System Threat Vector Taxonomy (arXiv:2511.21901), the open-source QUANTRRA Quantitative Risk Framework, and the book AI Management Systems: Operational Playbook for Chief AI Officers.
KEY DIFFERENTIATORS

    Quantitative AI Risk Pioneer: Designs Monte Carlo models that translate AI vulnerabilities into financial loss distributions, VaR, and risk-adjusted ROI for board decision-making
    Regulatory Compliance Authority: Operationalizes EU AI Act, ISO 42001, NIST AI RMF, and GDPR into auditable governance workflows and control matrices
    Algorithmic Auditing Specialist: Validates AI models for bias, drift, hallucinations, adversarial robustness, and security using Python, TensorFlow, PyTorch, Scikit-learn
    Global Thought Leader: Ranked Top 10 worldwide in AI Ethics and AI Governance by Thinkers360; featured speaker at KuppingerCole EIC, Risk Awareness Week, IIA conferences
    Executive Educator: Academic Director at IE Law School; delivers AI governance certifications and corporate training to multinational boards and C-suites

CURRENT ROLE

Capgemini | Senior Manager AI Governance & Digital Compliance | AI Risk Manager & Applied AI Lab Lead
January 2025 – Present | Copenhagen, Denmark

    Lead enterprise AI Governance and Responsible AI programs integrating algorithmic auditing, predictive risk models, and regulatory compliance (EU AI Act, NIS 2, GDPR, DORA)
    Direct Applied AI Lab (RIOT) developing compliant AI solutions for Fortune 500 clients across life sciences, defense, telecom, energy
    Architect GenAI strategies for SAP Joule, ServiceNow AI, enterprise Copilots transforming HR, Finance, GRC functions
    Design quantitative risk models using Monte Carlo simulation to measure AI financial exposure and ROI
    Advise C-suite on AI governance, algorithmic accountability, risk-based decision frameworks
    Conduct AI bias detection, adversarial testing, model validation using Python, TensorFlow, PyTorch, Scikit-learn
    Build ESG GRC automation solutions integrating SAP MDG, IoT sensors, data lakes for sustainability reporting (ISO 14001, 14064)
    Establish AI governance operating models for autonomous systems ensuring ISO 42001, EU AI Act compliance
    Validate AI clinical data automation for pharmaceutical trials using AWS Glue, ensuring patient safety and data integrity

PROFESSIONAL EXPERIENCE HIGHLIGHTS
Canon Group Milestone Systems

Head of Group Risk & Control | AI Risk Manager | Quantitative Risk Lead
August 2022 – November 2024 | Copenhagen, Denmark

    Designed Monte Carlo VaR models to quantify financial exposure of enterprise AI systems
    Built predictive risk algorithms using XGBoost, R, Python to forecast AI failures and anomalies
    Conducted algorithmic auditing pipelines stress-testing ML models for data drift, bias, adversarial vulnerabilities
    Developed quantitative frameworks translating AI uncertainties into financial metrics for Digital Compliance (NIS 2, EU AI Act, ISO 42001)
    Established cybersecurity and compliance controls ensuring SOX, FCPA, GDPR adherence

Danske Bank

IT Risk, GRC & Digital Compliance Senior Lead
June 2020 – August 2022 | Copenhagen, Denmark

    Led cyber risk and control program protecting bank-wide IT systems and information assets
    Assessed information security, cybersecurity, cloud services against ISO 27001, 27701, NIST 800-53, COBIT, EBA regulations
    Piloted centralized supplier due diligence compliance (EBA outsourcing guidelines, GDPR, ethical procurement)

ISS A/S

Head of Center of Excellence for Risk Management & Compliance
June 2018 – September 2019 | Copenhagen, Denmark

    Established global CoE for risk management, internal controls, compliance (Fortune Global 500)
    Integrated risk and control frameworks aligned to ISO 31000, GDPR, DPIA, ISO 27001
    Monitored risk treatment plans meeting Board requirements and multinational client compliance

Deloitte

Senior Manager Operational Risk & Risk Advisory
June 2017 – June 2018 | Copenhagen, Denmark

    Managed portfolio of risk and control consultancy projects across Deloitte North West Europe
    Delivered cybersecurity governance for global energy; internal control transformation for manufacturers; GDPR compliance for national banks; SOX process reviews

Veolia

Risk Management & Internal Controls Director
May 2011 – June 2017 | Madrid, Spain

    Led team of 14 risk and audit specialists across 80 subsidiaries in Iberia and Latin America
    Developed control self-assessments under ISO 31000, 19600, 37001, COSO, COBIT, GDPR
    Planned SOX 404 scope, testing, reporting; presented GRC solutions to CFO and senior management

Tenaris Techint

Compliance Audit Coordinator
August 2008 – September 2010 | International

    Developed corporate compliance program governing SEC, FCPA, SOX, US GAAP, IFRS, OFAC
    Supported SAP GRC and Business Intelligence initiatives; engineered automated transaction alerting

Baker Hughes

Business Process Support (SAP) & Compliance Auditor
April 2006 – June 2008 | Houston, Texas

    Coordinated SAP process design using ITIL; conducted SOX 404 audits globally
    Won Baker Hughes Core Value Award (Gold) for audit methodology innovation

ExxonMobil

Inventory & Accounting Compliance Specialist
March 2005 – April 2006 | Dallas, Texas

    Controlled finance migration to shared service center; assessed market, credit, operational trading risks

Deloitte

Senior Risk, IT, SOX Consultant | Senior Financial Auditor
January 2001 – March 2005

    Performed SOX, IT controls, operational audits using ACL; analyzed financial statements (IFRS, US GAAP)

    IIA Norway: "Model to Quantify Risks for Internal Auditors"
    IE Executive Education: "Invisible Correlations: Using Python and Network Analytics to Model Cascading Risks"
    ProcureCon Europe (WBR): Third-party AI risk and vendor due diligence keynote

PUBLICATIONS & RESEARCH
Books

AI Management Systems: Operational Playbook for Chief AI Officers and Compliance Risk Managers
ISBN-13: 9798233615009 | Google Play Books, Apple Books, Global Retailers

End-to-end operating system for AI governance translating EU AI Act, ISO 42001, NIST AI RMF into concrete board-level tasks. Introduces "Moneyball" approach replacing subjective heat maps with financial quantification of algorithmic bias, model drift, security failures. Covers lifecycle governance, AI Control Matrix, human-AI architectures, workforce psychology, automation anxiety.

GRC Framework: Governance for Risk and Compliance
Ediciones Roble

Conceptual foundation for enterprise GRC programs aligning board expectations, regulatory demands, operational execution under unified framework. Integrates ISO 31000, COSO, international compliance standards into pragmatic policy architectures, risk taxonomies, control libraries.
Research Papers

Standardized Threat Taxonomy for AI Security, Governance, and Regulatory Compliance
DOI: arXiv:2511.21901 | arXiv, AlphaXiv, Open Science Repositories

First rigorous bridge between technical AI vulnerabilities and financial risk quantification. Introduces AI System Threat Vector Taxonomy (9 Critical Domains, 133 real-world incidents). Maps threats to ISO 42001 controls, NIST AI RMF functions. Enables convolved Monte Carlo models for quantitative AI risk assessments including loss distributions, regulatory penalty scenarios, customer-churn impacts.

Quantitative Risk Assessment in R: An Open-Source Convolutional Framework for Modeling Uncertainty and Reserves
DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.17687261 | Zenodo

Free, open-source framework for quantitative risk assessment using Monte Carlo and convolution methods in R. Replaces simplistic risk matrices with mathematically sound probabilistic modeling. Executable R scripts run 100,000+ simulations producing risk statistics, contingency reserves, loss exceedance curves. Applicable to budgeting, legal claims, cyber risk, compliance exposure.
PROPRIETARY FRAMEWORKS & TOOLS
AI Management System Playbook & AI Control Accelerator

Proprietary AI Management System operationalizing AI governance for boards, CAIOs, GRC leaders. Translates EU AI Act, ISO 42001, NIST AI RMF into roles, workflows, layered controls. AI Control Matrix links real-time telemetry (drift, hallucinations, failures), impact thresholds, SLAs, contractual clauses to control owners and escalation paths. Covers full lifecycle governance from feasibility through deployment, monitoring, decommissioning.
AI System Threat Vector Taxonomy & Quantification Model

Semi-proprietary threat taxonomy codifying 9 AI threat domains (misuse, poisoning, prompt injection, hallucinations, data leakage, model theft, drift). Validated against 100+ real-world incidents. Mapped to ISO 42001 controls, NIST AI RMF. Quantitative Risk engine converts threat profiles into loss distributions via Monte Carlo, enabling translation into reserves, budgets, capital allocation, liability caps.
AI GRC Framework Datasets & Governance Ontology Library

Machine-readable AI GRC datasets published to Hugging Face encoding ISO 42001, 42005, 23894, 38507, 25059, EU AI Act, OWASP LLM Top 10, MITRE ATLAS, ENISA threat landscapes. Core tables: AI Risk Scenarios, AI Threat Vectors, AI Loss Taxonomy, AI Quality Objectives, AI Control Families. Ingestable into GRC platforms, risk registers, AI governance tools.
QUANTRRA Convolutional Quantitative Risk Framework (R & Python)

Open-source Quantitative Risk engine implementing compound frequency-severity models using Poisson frequency and lognormal severity via Monte Carlo and numerical convolution. Generates full loss distributions, reserves, exceedance curves, capital metrics. Optimized for commodity hardware/cloud notebooks. Includes parameter calibration, sensitivity analysis, stress testing for operational, cyber, legal, compliance scenarios.
Invisible Correlations Systemic Risk Index & Network Modeling Toolkit

Systemic AI and GRC risk methodology combining PCA, correlation analysis, network graphs to model cascading failures across AI systems, cyber assets, business processes, compliance obligations. Quantifies first-, second-, third-order impacts through shock propagation simulations. Produces composite Systemic Risk Index prioritizing interventions by resilience per unit cost.
Regression & AI Risk Modeling Suite (Python / Scikit-learn / TensorFlow)

Python notebooks applying regression and machine learning to predict compliance, legal, operational, cyber incidents from historical loss data. Includes GLM, tree-based methods, neural networks with governance guardrails (train/validation splits, error analysis, stability checks, fairness metrics, SHAP/LIME explainability). Enables predictive risk diagnostics, early-warning systems, targeted internal audit.
AI Risk Assessment & Corporate GPT Governance Toolkit

Practical assessment toolkit for internal GPT-style deployments and LLM-based assistants. Structured questionnaires, scenario libraries, quantitative templates evaluating prompt injection, data exfiltration, hallucination-driven decisions, unauthorized training data use. Includes impact assessment forms, RACI templates, script-based risk calculators mapping scenarios to business actors, attack vectors, controls, loss ranges.
AI-Aware Contract & SLA Clause Library

Structured library of contract clauses, KPIs, SLA patterns embedding AI Risk Management and Responsible AI obligations into commercial agreements. Covers model performance baselines, drift thresholds, explainability requirements, data-use rules, security controls, audit rights, indemnities, tiered liability caps linked to modeled loss distributions.
AI GRC Accelerator Package for Certifications

Modular AI GRC Accelerator bundling curricula, maturity models, role charters, risk appetite templates, control catalogues, assessment workflows, case studies for CAIO and Director of AI Governance programs. Covers AI Governance, AI Risk Management, Quantitative Risk, Responsible AI, Algorithmic Auditing, Digital Compliance. Functions as pedagogy asset and productized service.
Internal Audit & GRC Analytics Starter Kits (Python / R / Excel)

Lightweight analytics kits for internal audit, compliance, GRC teams adopting quantitative techniques. Parameterized scripts for sampling optimization, anomaly detection, control-failure simulation, portfolio risk aggregation, loss exceedance visualization. Built in Python, R with Excel front-ends. Map to assurance questions: audit prioritization, residual risk quantification, control effectiveness evidence.

TECHNICAL SKILLS
AI & Machine Learning Stack

Quantitative Risk & Analytics

Monte Carlo Simulation, Compound Poisson Lognormal Models, Value at Risk (VaR), Expected Shortfall, Loss Distribution Approach, Frequency-Severity Modeling, Backtesting, Stress Testing, Sensitivity Analysis, PCA, Network Risk Graphs, Regression (GLM, tree-based, neural networks), Jupyter Notebooks, GitHub

GRC & Compliance Frameworks

VALUE PROPOSITION FOR ORGANIZATIONS

Hernan Huwyler delivers AI governance that is board-ready, regulator-defensible, and operationally executable. Organizations hiring him gain:

    Quantitative Risk Translation: AI vulnerabilities converted into financial loss distributions, VaR, risk-adjusted ROI for capital allocation and board decisions
    Regulatory Compliance Certainty: EU AI Act, ISO 42001, NIST AI RMF, GDPR, DORA, NIS 2 operationalized into auditable workflows and control matrices
    Algorithmic Assurance: Python-based model validation for bias, drift, hallucinations, adversarial robustness satisfying internal audit and external regulators
    Enterprise Integration: AI governance embedded into existing GRC, ERM, SOX, cybersecurity, procurement, internal audit functions without organizational friction
    Executive Communication: Technical AI risks translated into board narratives, risk appetite statements, KRI dashboards, executive training that enable strategic oversight
    Thought Leadership Access: Proprietary frameworks (AI Control Matrix, QUANTRRA, Threat Taxonomy, Systemic Risk Index) accelerating implementation versus building from zero
    Global Credibility: Independent validation via Thinkers360 rankings, IE Law School academic authority, KuppingerCole speaker credentials, Copenhagen Compliance certification leadership

IDEAL ENGAGEMENT TYPES

    Chief AI Officer Advisory: Board-ready AI governance operating models, risk appetite frameworks, AI committee charters
    AI Risk Quantification Projects: Monte Carlo models translating AI threats into financial reserves, capital allocation, liability caps
    Algorithmic Auditing Engagements: Python-based validation of ML models for bias, drift, security, explainability (internal audit, regulatory submission)
    EU AI Act Compliance Programs: High-risk AI system classification, technical documentation, conformity assessment, post-market surveillance
    Executive Training & Workshops: CAIO certification, AI governance for boards, quantitative risk modeling, algorithmic auditing bootcamps
    Keynote Speaking: Conferences on AI governance, responsible AI, quantitative risk, GRC transformation, digital compliance
    Technology Risk Assessments: AI vendor due diligence, shadow AI detection, AI procurement frameworks, third-party model evaluation
    Internal Audit Transformation: AI system audit universe optimization, risk-based planning, continuous monitoring for AI controls
